LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT DAVID DUPREE'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF JAVIER POYATOS HAS SHOWN THAT A SUBSTANTIAL PORTION OF THE TIME FROM THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND THE ENTRY OF THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS TOLLED DUE TO THE MISCONDUCT OF MR. LAUB IN ABANDONING MR. POYATOS. ONCE THE TOLLED TIME IS REMOVED, IT REMAINS POSSIBLE FOR THIS CASE TO BE TRIED WITHIN THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 583.310. DUE TO THE MISCONDUCT OF MR. LAUB, THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THIS IS NOT AN APPROPRIATE CASE TO BE DISMISSED UNDER THE DISCRETIONARY DISMISSAL STATUTE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action
Off Calendar Apr-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID DUPREE'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION.

Appearances: Melissa L. O'Connor of Bogaards Davis LLP (415.979.0480) for defendant and moving party; Sandra Ribera Speed of the Ribera Law Firm (415.576.1600) for plaintiff and opposing party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. Plaintiff Javier Poyatos has shown that a substantial portion of the time from the entry of default and the entry of the default judgment is tolled due to the misconduct of Mr. Laub in abandoning Mr. Poyatos. Once the tolled time is removed, it remains possible for this case to be tried within the requirements of CCP 583.310. Due to the misconduct of Mr. Laub, the court believes that this is not an appropriate case to be dismissed under the discretionary dismissal statute. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. SANDRA RIBERA SPEED & ATTY. JILL LIN OF THE RIBERA LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-576-1600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MELISSA L. O'CONNER OF THE BOGAARDS DAVIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-979-0480 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The court denied the request to present oral testimony. DEFENDANT DAVID DUPREE'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Set Aside Default is granted. Defendant is entitled to relief pursuant to CCP 473.5. The motion is timely, defendant provides a proposed answer, and defendant demonstrates a lack of actual notice. Mr. Dupree declares that he did not learn of this case until the insurer informed him of the judgment. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
